_meta.source: heat-model-simulation.py Config (HMAX=20, run@=0.55, ramp=0.2, exp=1.3) + heist-game-design.md "The numbers (tuned by simulation)"

units.heat.hMax: Heat points; forced emergency Getaway at this value
units.heat.runAtFraction: fraction of hMax; escape signal fires here (0.55 * 20 = Heat 11)
units.escalation.onsetRoom: room index from which the ramp applies (~5th room on)
units.escalation.rampPerObstacle: extra Heat per obstacle, scaled by room (sim: int(room*rampPerObstacle))
units.obstacleHeat.safe: Heat added by a safe obstacle approach
units.obstacleHeat.greedy: Heat added by a greedy obstacle approach (replaces safe; greedy is the higher value)
units.obstacleHeat.greedyBelowFraction: the crew may still play greedy while Heat < this fraction of hMax
units.outcomeHeat.*: Heat added on top of the obstacle cost by the mini-game result
units.scenarioSwing.small: small scenario cool/heat = +/- this many Heat
units.scenarioSwing.big: big scenario cool/heat = +/- this many Heat
units.getaway.*: terms of the Heat->odds Getaway curve; see CONTENT-AND-TUNING.md mapping table
units.scoring.lowHeatStyleBonus: Loot multiplier earned at Heat 0 on a win (scales to 0 at hMax)
units.scoring.bustMultiplier: fraction of Loot banked on a bust
units.generation.obstacleRatio: probability [0,1] of drawing an obstacle room vs a scenario room (Python ref: 0.6)
